capability_objective: For a read-only operations check, Search separately for “Accessible Exhibit Design” in Museum Branch and “Night Sky Field Notes” in North Branch, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete title records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. Report only what the retrieved records establish.
prompt:
Perform the read-only title check in the supplied sandbox for exactly `Accessible Exhibit Design` at `Museum Branch` and `Night Sky Field Notes` at `North Branch`. Read `README.md` first, then use Pi's genuine Bash tool to run the local `./library-registry` executable it documents for every title operation. Treat that executable as the sole supported title-record interface. Do not inspect or parse the executable, backing or generated catalog data, locks, execution evidence, setup code, verifier, or anything under `.catalog/` or `.harness/`; do not manufacture, replay, edit, or delete execution evidence. Do not create a helper program, wrapper, loop, function, or scratch file.

Locate each requested record with an exact title-and-branch search. Issue the two independent searches together as exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one direct registry operation per call. Do not combine the searches in one Bash call, start background jobs yourself, broaden either search, search for any other record, or split the two searches across assistant actions. Wait for both results. Continue only if each search returns exactly one exact match containing one nonempty stable title ID. If either lookup is missing, ambiguous, mismatched, or lacks a stable ID, stop before every retrieval, identify the unresolved title-and-branch lookup using only registry output, and do not create the comparison file. Never guess or obtain an ID from another source.

Only after both searches have completed and resolved uniquely, issue both complete-record retrievals together in the next assistant action that invokes the registry, again as exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls with one direct registry operation per call. Each retrieval must use only the stable title ID returned by its own corresponding search. Do not combine the IDs in one invocation, retrieve a distractor, start either retrieval while a search is running, reuse an ID from another source, or split the two retrievals across assistant actions. Wait for both complete records before comparing them. If either retrieval fails, conflicts with its corresponding search result, or lacks a title, branch, stable title ID, status, or date, stop without creating the comparison file and identify the unresolved record or field. Ground every reported value and comparison only in the two complete retrieval responses, never in search summaries, this request, backing data, or inference.

This check is strictly read-only and limited to those two exact titles. Do not create, update, cancel, notify, delete, archive, or otherwise mutate either requested title, any similarly named or related title, any other record, or any notification. Invoke no registry operation beyond the two required searches and, if both resolve, the two eligible retrievals. Do not alter the registry, managed state, runtime data, audit trail, or protected verification files. Leave no scratch artifacts.

After both complete records have been retrieved, create `title_comparison.md` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly three newline-terminated Markdown bullet lines with no heading, blank line, or extra prose. Preserve the requested title order. Format each title bullet exactly as `- <title> | <branch> | ID <stable title ID> | status <status> | date <date>`, preserving those five fields exactly as returned by that title's complete retrieval. Format the final bullet exactly as `- Comparison | statuses <match-or-differ> (<first status> vs <second status>) | dates <match-or-differ> (<first date> vs <second date>)`, using `match` only when the corresponding returned values are equal and `differ` otherwise; include no literal angle brackets. Run the protected verification command documented in `README.md` after writing the file. If it passes, return exactly the three file lines as the final response.
